zh.Halberton, Devon: 英國英格蘭戴文哈爾波頓
zh.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee是Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee的兒子，於1562年8月出生在英國英格蘭戴文武爾。他於1592年5月7日與Alice Cannone Keeper在英國英格蘭戴文武爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他去世於1638年在他的家鄉享年76歲。

zh-hans.Halberton, Devon: 英国英格兰戴文哈爾波頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee是Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee的儿子，于1562年8月出生在英国英格兰戴文武爾。他于1592年5月7日与Alice Cannone Keeper在英国英格兰戴文武爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他去世于1638年在他的家乡享年76岁。

nameMeanings.name: Keeper
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German   Kuper   1.

English (London): occupational name from Middle English keper ‘guardian, warden, custodian’, or a topographic name from Middle English keper, either ‘dweller by the castle keep’ or ‘dweller by the look-out place’. See Keep for the senses.

variants.name: Kuper
meanings: North German (also Küper): occupational name for a cooper, from Middle Low German kuper ‘cooper’.
Swiss German: nickname for a sulky person, from Swiss German kupen ‘to sulk, to pout’.
Jewish (eastern Ashkenazic): from Yiddish kuper ‘copper’ (or a Yiddishized form of German Kupfer), hence a metonymic occupational name for a worker or trader in copper or an artificial name.

nameMeanings.name: AKA
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Japanese Masao, Takashi, Kaoru, Makiko, Masahiko, Rieko, Shigeki, Shogo, Akio, Atsuhiko, Atsushi, Haruo.
Japanese: written 岡 or 丘 ‘hill’. It is found mostly in western Japan. In North America, the name could also be a shortening of longer names beginning with Oka-.
Albanian: metonymic occupational name from okë (definite form oka) ‘oke’, the name of an old measure of weight.

nameMeanings.name: Park,
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Sang, Sung, Yong, Jung, Jong, Chan, Jae, Jin, Kwang, Kyung, Myung, Chong, Chang, Chung, Byung, Min, Moon, Chul, Hae, Jeong, Dae, Seong, Myong.

English and Scottish: from Middle English and Older Scots parc, parke, perk ‘park, enclosure’ (Old French parc), often referring to the grounds of a manor house or a deer park where the lord hunted. The name was probably synonymous with Parker , denoting an officer who looked after a park for the lord of the manor.
English and Scottish: from a medieval pet form of the personal name   Peter  . Compare   Parkin  .

variants.name: Parker
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English parker ‘park-keeper’ (Old French parquier, parchier), an officer employed to look after deer and other game in a hunting park (see Park 1). This surname is also very common among African Americans. It has also been recorded since medieval times in Ireland.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

variants.name: Pak
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Chong, Yong, Young, Sung, Song, Sang, Kyong, Kwang, Chun, Chang, Sun, Jong, Jung, Chan, Chung, Byung, Nam, Myong, Pyong, Chol, Hae, Hyong, In Kyu, Tuk.

Korean: written 박, in Chinese characters 朴, meaning ‘simple’; this is the third most common surname in Korea and is found in every part of the peninsula. The Pak clans claim no founding ancestors from foreign lands - all are native to Korea. Although some sources indicate that there are 270 Pak clans, only 44 of these can be documented, and much of their early history is shrouded in myth and legend. All Pak clans spring from a single semi-mythological ancestor named Pak Hyŏkkŏse (朴 赫; 居世). Before the establishment of the Shilla kingdom the southern tip of the peninsula is said to have been ruled by six elders, leaders of the six tribes who inhabited that part of the country. In AD 69 a leader, Pak Hyŏkkŏse, arrived at the foot of a mountain in Kyŏngju in Kyŏngsang north province. Legend has it that he was inside a golden gourd-shaped egg. When the egg was opened, Pak Hyŏkkŏse emerged and, after taking a wife (who was borne on the back of a dragon), he established the new Shilla kingdom. The word pak in Korean (박; 朴) can mean either ‘gourd’ or ‘to shine brightly’, and the surname Pak is said to denote either the gourd-shaped egg in which the youth was found or the light that is said to have radiated from his head. Compare Park .
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 白 and 柏, see   Bai   1 and 2.

variants.name: Parks
meanings: English:
variant of Park 1 with genitival, plural, or post-medieval excrescent -s.
variant of Perk , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Parrack
meanings: English: topographic name or nickname from Middle English par(r)o(c)k ‘enclosed pasture, paddock’ (Old English pearroc), signifying one who lived or worked at a paddock; there is sometimes later confusion with Park .

variants.name: Parke
meanings: English: variant of   Park  . This form of the surname is common in Ireland.

nameMeanings.name: Parker
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English parker ‘park-keeper’ (Old French parquier, parchier), an officer employed to look after deer and other game in a hunting park (see Park 1). This surname is also very common among African Americans. It has also been recorded since medieval times in Ireland.
Americanized form of one or more similar (like-sounding) Jewish names.

variants.name: Parkman
meanings: English:

occupational name from Middle English park ‘enclosure, game preserve, park’ (Old French parc) + man, sometimes for one living at or near such a place but probably more often denoting someone employed to look after after deer and other game in a hunting park; compare Parker .
occupational name denoting the servant (Middle English man) of someone called Park (see Park 2).

variants.name: Parkerson
meanings: English: from the occupational name Parker + patronymic -son.

variants.name: Parkey
meanings: English (Cheshire and Lancashire): dialect pronunciation of Parker . Parker has a long history in Macclesfield, Manchester, and Bridgnorth.

variants.name: Park
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Korean Young, Sang, Sung, Yong, Jung, Jong, Chan, Jae, Jin, Kwang, Kyung, Myung, Chong, Chang, Chung, Byung, Min, Moon, Chul, Hae, Jeong, Dae, Seong, Myong.

English and Scottish: from Middle English and Older Scots parc, parke, perk ‘park, enclosure’ (Old French parc), often referring to the grounds of a manor house or a deer park where the lord hunted. The name was probably synonymous with Parker , denoting an officer who looked after a park for the lord of the manor.
English and Scottish: from a medieval pet form of the personal name   Peter  . Compare   Parkin  .

nameMeanings.name: and
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Jasvir, Dalbir, Jagdish.  German Alois, Ewald, Gerda.
Indian (Punjab): Sikh name, probably from Punjabi məṇḍ ‘skill’. It is based on the name of a Jat clan.
German: variant of   Mandt  .

variants.name: Mandt
meanings: German and Norwegian (of Danish origin): from a short form of the ancient Germanic personal names beginning with Mant-, e.g. Mantwic, formed with Old High German mandjan ‘rejoice’ as the first element.

variants.name: Mander
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Balwinder, Bikram.
English: occupational name from Middle English mander, maunder ‘basket-maker’, a derivative of Middle English mande, maunde ‘basket’. Compare Mand .
German: habitational name from any of the places called Mandern, in Hesse and the Rhineland.

nameMeanings.name: Lee
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sang, Jae, Jong, Jung, Sung, Yong, Kyung, Seung, Dong, Kwang, Myung.

English: topographic name for someone who lived near a meadow or a patch of arable land, Middle English lee, lea, from Old English lēa, dative case (used after a preposition) of lēah, which originally meant ‘wood or glade’.

English: habitational name from any of the many places in England named with Old English lēah ‘wood, glade’, including Lee in Buckinghamshire, Essex, Hampshire, Kent, and Shropshire, and Lea in Cheshire, Derbyshire, Herefordshire, Lancashire, Lincolnshire, and Wiltshire.

variants.name: Ley
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
German: from a medieval personal name, Eloy, from Latin Eligius (see Eligio ), a Christian saint's name.
German (Rhineland): topographic name from Middle High German leie ‘rock, stone, slate’, or a habitational name from any of several places called with this word. Compare Leier .

variants.name: Lees
meanings: English and Scottish: topographic name from Middle English lees ‘fields, arable land’, plural of lee (see Lee ), or from Middle English lese ‘pasture, meadow’ (Old English lǣs).

English: habitational name fromLeece or Lees in Lancashire, or Leese in Cheshire, all named from OldEnglish lēas ‘woodland clearings’ (plural oflēah), or from Leece in Cumbria, which was probably namedwith a Celtic word, lïss ‘hall, court, the principalhouse in a district’.
English: variant of   Leece   1.

variants.name: Lay
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Scottish: shortened form of   McClay  .
German: habitational name from any of the places so named, in the Rhineland near Koblenz and in Bavaria, from lay(h), a word meaning ‘stone, rock, slate’.

variants.name: Laye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

variants.name: Lie
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Scandinavian Erik, Oystein, Sven, Thor.  German Hans, Gunther, Hilde.  Korean Han, Chang Ho, Chen, Chun, Hok, Jung, Kam, Liang, Lie, Ling, Shuen, Yung.
Norwegian: variant of Li 11, a habitational name from any of numerous farmsteads named Li, from Old Norse hlíth ‘mountain slope, hillside’. In Norway this is the most frequent spelling.
German: unexplained.

variants.name: Layman
meanings: English: topographic name for a dweller by a woodland clearing or a meadow, from Middle English ley + man. Compare Lee .
English: nickname from layman, a word denoting an uneducated person.
Americanized form of German   Lehmann  .

variants.name: Lea
meanings: English: variant of   Lee  .
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surnames 李 and 利, see   Li   1 and 3.

variants.name: Lye
meanings: English: variant of   Lee   1, a topographic name for someone who lived near a patch of arable land, or a habitational name from Lye in Herefordshire (with the same etymology).

Norwegian: habitational name from a farmstead in Rogaland named Lye, Old Norse Lýgi meaning ‘alliance, covenant’, used to denote a place sanctified by such an agreement, such as a court or council meeting place.
Altered form of German   Ley  .

variants.name: Saelee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Southeast Asian Chan, Kao, Nai, Chai, Cheng, Chien, Foo, Hin, Muang, Pao, Seng, Song, Sou.
Southeast Asian (Iu Mien): from the name of the Lee clan of the Iu Mien people from Laos + the Thai prefix sae, denoting a clan. Compare Lee 8.

variants.name: Leigh
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places (in at least sixteen counties, but especially Leigh in Lancashire) named either with the nominative case of Old English lēah ‘woodland clearing’ (see Lee ) or with lēage, a late dative form of this word (see Lye ).
Chinese: variant Romanization of the surname 李, based on its Cantonese pronunciation. See   Li   1.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee was born in August 1562, in Uffculme, Devon, England, United Kingdom as the son of Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. He married Alice Cannone Keeper on 7 May 1592, in Uffculme, Devon, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. He died in 1638, in his hometown, at the age of 76.

fr.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee est né en août 1562, à Uffculme, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, fils de Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. Il a épousé Alice Cannone Keeper le 7 mai 1592, à Uffculme, Devon, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Il est décédé en 1638, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 76 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee wurde im August 1562 in Uffculme, Devon, England, United Kingdom, als Sohn von Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee geboren. Er hat am 7. Mai 1592 in Uffculme, Devon, England, United Kingdom Alice Cannone Keeper geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. Er starb 1638 im Alter von 76 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee è nato il nel agosto 1562 nel Uffculme, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito come figlio di Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. Lui sposato Alice Cannone Keeper il 7 maggio 1592, nel Uffculme, Devon, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. Morì nel 1638, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 76 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee(은)는 1562년 8월 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 프컬에서 Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1592년 5월 7일 영국 잉글랜드 데폰 프컬에서 Alice Cannone Keeper(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1638년 그의 고향에서 76세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee nasceu em agosto de 1562, em Uffculme, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom filho de Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. Ele casou-se com Alice Cannone Keeper em 7 de maio de 1592, em Uffculme, Devon, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Ele faleceu em 1638, em sua cidade natal, com 76 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee родился август 1562 г. в местности уффкулм, девон, Англия, Великобритания. Его отца звали Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice Cannone Keeper 7 мая 1592 г. в местности уффкулм, девон, Англия, Великобритания. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь. Он умер в 1638 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 76 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Thomas Keeper   AKA Park, Parker and Lee nació en agosto de 1562, en Uffculme, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido como hijo de Richard   Worsley father Parker   alias Keeper also Richard Park alias Lee. Se casó con Alice Cannone Keeper el 7 de mayo de 1592, en Uffculme, Devon, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Murió en 1638, en su pueblo, a la edad de 76 años.
